docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Dagflow runs Dagster OSS entirely inside a consumer Snowflake account and security boundary using Snowpark Container Services. The Native App creates the Dagster control plane, code-location services, and autoscaling short-lived run-worker job services.

Dagflow integrates Dagster with Snowflake authentication, RBAC, image repositories, external access integrations, and secrets.

These docs are for:

  • Admins who install Dagflow, grant roles, add code locations, and operate the app.
  • Users who use the Dagster UI or APIs.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This website documents how to install, configure, and operate Dagflow. It does not replace the official Dagster documentation for Dagster APIs, definitions, assets, jobs, schedules, sensors, or general Dagster concepts.

For Dagster-specific usage, see the official Dagster documentation.

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Install or uninstall the Dagflow Native App.
  • Add or remove code locations.
  • Control app access with application roles.
  • Launch Dagster runs.
  • Update code locations.

docs.title: Where To Start
docs.text:
• Admins: Install Dagflow, then App API and Operations.
  • Users: RBAC, then Snowflake Run Tags.

docs.title: App API and Operations
docs.text: App management procedures live in <app_name>.app_public. APP_ADMIN can call all procedures on this page. APP_EDITOR can call only update_code_location_image.

docs.title: start_app
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    start_app()
    Creates the Dagflow compute pools and starts or updates the core.postgres and core.dagster services.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.start_app();

docs.title: grant_callback
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    grant_callback(privileges ARRAY)
    Retries the same startup callback that Snowflake invokes when required app privileges are granted. Use this only when startup did not happen automatically after granting CREATE COMPUTE POOL and BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description privileges Yes Array of granted privilege names. The app starts only when the array contains both CREATE COMPUTE POOL and BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.grant_callback([
  'CREATE COMPUTE POOL',
  'BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT'
]);

docs.title: service_status
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    service_status()
    Shows services in the app core schema.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_status();

docs.title: dagster_containers
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    dagster_containers()
    Shows containers for the core.dagster service.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.dagster_containers();

docs.title: dagster_logs
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    dagster_logs(
  container_name STRING,
  line_count NUMBER
)
    Returns logs from a core.dagster container.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description container_name Yes Dagster service container name. Use webserver or daemon. line_count No Number of log lines to return. Defaults to 100 when NULL.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.dagster_logs('webserver', 200);
CALL dagflow_app.app_public.dagster_logs('daemon', NULL);

docs.title: add_code_location
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    add_code_location(
  code_location_name STRING,
  blocking BOOLEAN
)
    Creates a new code-location service in the app CODE_LOCATIONS schema with Dagflow's bundled placeholder image. This is a create-only operation and fails if the service already exists. Pass FALSE to return after requesting service creation, or TRUE to wait for readiness. Use update_code_location_image to provide the real user code-server image.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description code_location_name Yes Code location name. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores. Dagflow stores it uppercase. blocking Yes When TRUE, waits up to 5 minutes for the placeholder code-server container to report READY. When FALSE or NULL, returns after requesting service creation.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.add_code_location('finance', FALSE);

Then provide the real user code-server image:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-06-01',
  'finance.definitions',
  FALSE
);

Use blocking when automation should fail if the placeholder code server does not become ready:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.add_code_location(
  'finance',
  TRUE
);

docs.title: update_code_location_image
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    update_code_location_image(
  code_location_name STRING,
  image STRING,
  module_name STRING,
  blocking BOOLEAN
)
    Updates the image and module name for an existing code-location service. Pass FALSE to return after requesting service update, or TRUE to wait for readiness.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description code_location_name Yes Existing code location name. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores. image Yes New Snowflake image repository path. Empty strings are not allowed. module_name Yes Python module containing Dagster definitions in the new image, for example finance.definitions. blocking Yes When TRUE, waits up to 5 minutes for all code-server containers to report READY. When FALSE or NULL, returns after requesting service update.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN or APP_EDITOR.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-06-01',
  'finance.definitions',
  FALSE
);
    Use blocking when automation should fail if the updated code-server container does not become ready: 
    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-06-01',
  'finance.definitions',
  TRUE
);

docs.title: remove_code_location
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    remove_code_location(code_location_name STRING)
    Drops an existing code-location service. This removes it from Dagster after workspace refresh.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description code_location_name Yes Code location name to drop. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.remove_code_location('finance');
    Dagflow uses the registered code-server image as the run-worker image, so the image must include dagster-postgres and the dependencies needed to execute runs.

docs.title: restart_dagster
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    restart_dagster()
    Suspends and resumes the core.dagster service. Use this when the control plane needs a manual restart.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.restart_dagster();

docs.title: suspend_services
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    suspend_services()
    Suspends the core.dagster and core.postgres services. Compute pools are left running.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.suspend_services();

docs.title: suspend_compute_pools
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    suspend_compute_pools()
    Suspends the app control, code-location, and run compute pools. Services are not explicitly suspended first.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.suspend_compute_pools();

docs.title: suspend_app
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    suspend_app()
    Suspends core.dagster, core.postgres, and all Dagflow compute pools.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.suspend_app();

docs.title: service_containers
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    service_containers(service_name STRING)
    Shows containers for a service in the app core schema.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description service_name Yes Core service name without schema, for example dagster or postgres. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_containers('dagster');

docs.title: service_logs
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    service_logs(
  service_name STRING,
  container_name STRING,
  line_count NUMBER
)
    Returns logs from a service container in the app core schema.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description service_name Yes Core service name without schema, for example dagster or postgres. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores. container_name Yes Container name. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens. line_count No Number of log lines to return. Defaults to 100 when NULL.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_logs('dagster', 'webserver', 500);
CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_logs('postgres', 'postgres', NULL);
    Use these procedures when the UI is unavailable, a code update is missing, or Snowflake reports an unhealthy service.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
You need a Snowflake role that can install the Dagflow Native App and grant its requested privileges. The app needs account-level privileges to create compute pools and bind service endpoints.

You also need a Snowflake image repository with your Dagster code-server image. Dagflow uses the same image for run workers, so it must include your project code and the dependencies needed by both the gRPC code server and dagster api execute_run.

docs.title: Install the App
docs.text:
If you install from the Snowflake Marketplace UI, follow the UI prompts and skip this section. To run the same steps manually, continue below.

After installation, grant requested privileges if they were not auto-granted:

GRANT CREATE COMPUTE POOL ON ACCOUNT TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT ON ACCOUNT TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

Snowflake invokes the app grant callback after required privileges are granted. To retry startup manually:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.start_app();
CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_status();

Grant Dagflow application roles to users or roles that need the Dagster UI:

GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_VIEWER TO ROLE <viewer_role>;
GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_LAUNCHER TO ROLE <launcher_role>;
GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_EDITOR TO ROLE <editor_role>;
GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_ADMIN TO ROLE <admin_role>;

Use the least powerful role that fits each user. See RBAC for allowed actions.

docs.title: Prepare a Code Image Repository
docs.text:
Create an image repository for containers with your Dagster definition code, then grant Dagflow access:

CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB;
CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.CODE;
CREATE IMAGE REPOSITORY IF NOT EXISTS DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.CODE.CONSUMER_REPO;

GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.CODE TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT READ ON IMAGE REPOSITORY DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.CODE.CONSUMER_REPO
  TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

Build and push your Dagster code image to that repository. The image must expose the Python module with your Dagster definitions and include dagster-postgres, because run workers use Dagflow's PostgreSQL-backed Dagster storage.

docs.title: Add a Code Location
docs.text:
Add the code-location service:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.add_code_location('finance', FALSE);

Dagflow creates an app-owned code location named FINANCE with its bundled placeholder image. Provide the real user code-server image with update_code_location_image:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-05-29',
  'finance.definitions',
  FALSE
);

If the real image is not ready yet, an APP_ADMIN can add the location first and run update_code_location_image later.

Pass TRUE for blocking when automation should wait for readiness:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-05-29',
  'finance.definitions',
  TRUE
);

After the service is ready, open the Dagster UI from the Snowflake service endpoint for core.dagster.

docs.title: Compute Resources
docs.text:
Dagflow creates three SPCS compute pools in the consumer account:

Compute pool Used for Instance family Nodes <app_name>_CONTROL_POOL Dagster UI, daemon, and storage GEN_ARM_G1_8 1 <app_name>_CODE_LOCATION_POOL Registered code locations GEN_ARM_G1_4 1-20 <app_name>_RUN_POOL Pipeline run workers GEN_ARM_G1_8 1-50

Run workers default to 0.25 vCPU and 512Mi memory per run. Users can request different resources with snowflake/cpu and snowflake/memory tags.

docs.title: RBAC
docs.text: Dagflow uses Snowflake application roles to control Dagster operations. Admins grant these roles to Snowflake account roles; users do not manage them directly.

docs.title: Roles
Choosing Roles
docs.text:
• Give APP_VIEWER to users who inspect jobs, assets, run history, and logs.
  • Give APP_LAUNCHER to users who launch or stop runs.
  • Give APP_EDITOR to users who manage schedules, sensors, backfills, concurrency, or existing code-location images.
  • Give APP_ADMIN only to Dagflow operators.

docs.title: Operation Mapping
docs.text:
Roles are hierarchical. APP_EDITOR includes APP_LAUNCHER; APP_ADMIN includes all permissions below.

Operation Required role launchRun APP_LAUNCHER launchPipelineExecution APP_LAUNCHER launchRunReexecution APP_LAUNCHER launchPipelineReexecution APP_LAUNCHER terminateRun APP_LAUNCHER terminatePipelineExecution APP_LAUNCHER terminateRuns APP_LAUNCHER deleteRun APP_LAUNCHER deletePipelineRun APP_LAUNCHER startSchedule APP_EDITOR stopRunningSchedule APP_EDITOR resetSchedule APP_EDITOR scheduleDryRun APP_EDITOR startSensor APP_EDITOR stopSensor APP_EDITOR resetSensor APP_EDITOR setSensorCursor APP_EDITOR sensorDryRun APP_EDITOR wipeAssets APP_EDITOR reportRunlessAssetEvents APP_EDITOR setAutoMaterializePaused APP_EDITOR launchPartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR launchBackfill APP_EDITOR cancelPartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR resumePartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR reexecutePartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR addDynamicPartition APP_EDITOR deleteDynamicPartitions APP_EDITOR launchMultipleRuns APP_EDITOR setConcurrencyLimit APP_EDITOR deleteConcurrencyLimit APP_EDITOR freeConcurrencySlotsForRun APP_EDITOR freeConcurrencySlots APP_EDITOR reloadRepositoryLocation APP_ADMIN reloadWorkspace APP_ADMIN shutdownRepositoryLocation APP_ADMIN

docs.title: Snowflake Run Tags
docs.text: This page is for users who launch runs and admins who grant Snowflake access. Set these Dagster run tags only when a run needs custom worker resources, external access integrations, or Snowflake secrets.

docs.title: CPU and Memory
docs.text:
Use snowflake/cpu and snowflake/memory to request CPU and memory for one run.

By default, run workers use 0.25 vCPU and 512Mi memory. This matches Dagster ECS' default Linux run-task resources. If dagster.yaml sets launcher-level run_resources, these tags override them for the run.

Example:

{
  "snowflake/cpu": "0.5",
  "snowflake/memory": "2Gi"
}

CPU values must be positive vCPU quantities such as 0.5, 1, or 500m. Memory values must include a Snowflake-supported unit: M, Mi, G, or Gi.

docs.title: External Access Integrations
docs.text:
Use snowflake/external_access_integrations when a run needs outbound network access allowed by a Snowflake external access integration.

The value can be a comma-separated string:

OPENAI_EAI,SLACK_EAI

Or a JSON string array:

["OPENAI_EAI", "SLACK_EAI"]

Each value must be an unquoted Snowflake identifier.

The installed application must have USAGE on every integration used by the run:

GRANT USAGE ON INTEGRATION OPENAI_EAI TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
Use snowflake/secrets when a run needs Snowflake secrets as environment variables. The value must be a JSON string array:

[
  {
    "snowflakeSecret": "DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE.API_TOKEN",
    "secretKeyRef": "secret_string",
    "envVarName": "MY_API_KEY"
  }
]

Fields:

  • snowflakeSecret: Snowflake secret name, unqualified or dotted, such as DATABASE.SCHEMA.SECRET_NAME.
  • secretKeyRef: key exposed by the Snowflake secret, such as secret_string or password.
  • envVarName: environment variable name inside the run worker.

The installed application needs USAGE on the secret schema and READ on each secret:

GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT READ ON SECRET DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE.API_TOKEN
  TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

Secret injection is independent from external access integrations. If a run sets both tags, Snowflake validates whether the integration allows the selected secrets.

docs.title: Example Dagster Launch Tags
docs.text:
When launching a run through Dagster GraphQL or any Dagster API that accepts execution metadata, pass tags as strings:

[
  {
    "key": "snowflake/cpu",
    "value": "0.5"
  },
  {
    "key": "snowflake/memory",
    "value": "2Gi"
  },
  {
    "key": "snowflake/external_access_integrations",
    "value": "OPENAI_EAI"
  },
  {
    "key": "snowflake/secrets",
    "value": "[{\"snowflakeSecret\":\"DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE.API_TOKEN\",\"secretKeyRef\":\"secret_string\",\"envVarName\":\"MY_API_KEY\"}]"
  }
]

Dagflow validates tag values before creating the run-worker job service. Invalid resources, identifiers, JSON, duplicate environment variable names, or missing required fields fail the launch.

docs.title: Code Location
docs.text:
Dagster separates the control plane from user code. See the Dagster code locations architecture for details.

To update a code location, rebuild the Docker image with your Dagster definitions and push it to the Snowflake SPCS image registry. Dagflow uses the registered code-server image as the run-worker image for runs from that code location, so the image must include dagster-postgres as well as your project dependencies.

docs.title: Build a Code Location Image
docs.text:
A code-location image should contain your Dagster definitions module, Dagster, dagster-postgres, and any Python or system dependencies your jobs need at run time.

Example project layout:

.
|-- Dockerfile
|-- requirements.txt
`-- finance
    |-- __init__.py
    `-- definitions.py

Example requirements.txt:

dagster==1.12.1
dagster-postgres==0.28.1
snowflake-connector-python>=3.15,<4

Example Dockerfile:

FROM python:3.11-slim

ENV DAGSTER_HOME=/opt/dagster/app \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1

WORKDIR /opt/dagster/app

COPY requirements.txt ./
RUN pip install --upgrade pip \
    && pip install -r requirements.txt

COPY finance ./finance

EXPOSE 4000

CMD ["dagster", "api", "grpc", "--module-name", "finance.definitions", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "4000"]

Build and push the image to the Snowflake image repository that the Dagflow app can read:

snow spcs image-registry login

docker buildx build --platform linux/arm64 \
  -t <registry>/finance-dagster:2026-06-01 \
  --push .

Use the Snowflake image path when adding or updating the code location:

/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-06-01

docs.title: Update an Existing Code Location
docs.text:
After an APP_ADMIN adds a code location, an APP_EDITOR can update code by pushing a new image version and calling:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-06-01',
  'finance.definitions',
  FALSE
);

Pass TRUE for blocking when a release script should fail if the new code server does not become ready:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.update_code_location_image(
  'finance',
  '/DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB/CODE/CONSUMER_REPO/finance-dagster:2026-06-01',
  'finance.definitions',
  TRUE
);

docs.title: Refresh Dagster
docs.text:
Updating an existing code-location image does not require reinstalling Dagflow. If Dagster does not show the latest code, reload the code location in the Dagster UI.

Adding or removing a code location changes the Snowflake service list. The webserver and daemon refresh their workspace from running services in the code-location schema, so add_code_location and remove_code_location do not restart core.dagster.

Restart the control plane manually when needed:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.restart_dagster();

docs.title: Remove a Code Location
docs.text:
Remove code locations that should no longer appear in Dagster:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.remove_code_location('finance');

Dagflow drops the code-location service. The webserver and daemon stop listing it after workspace refresh.
